title: Ania borneensis
taxonomy: Ania borneensis is a genus and species of moth in the family Erebidae, specifically within the subfamily Arctiinae. The classification of Ania borneensis places it within the kingdom Animalia, phylum Arthropoda, and class Insecta. It belongs to the order Lepidoptera and has a more specific classification within the family Erebidae. The subfamily Arctiinae, to which Ania borneensis belongs, consists of various species of moths commonly known as tiger moths or woolly bears. The classification of Ania borneensis is a crucial aspect of understanding its biology and behavior.
uses: Ania borneensis is a plant species native to Borneo, and its medicinal uses are still being researched. There is limited information available on the specific medicinal properties of Ania borneensis, but it is believed to have potential in traditional medicine.
